editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter of congratulation on the golden wedding anniversary of Lord and Lady Clonbrock.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Yrs v. affectn.May Mackenzie
Inverness Station (9 Miles)
Parcels to Daviot Station (7 Miles) Inverfarigaig Pier for Canal 13 Miles
(conveyances at Foyers Hotel)
Letters and Telegrams to
HOUSE OF FARR,
INVERNESS.
25: July. My dear Tweetie I was very much interested
in seeing the other day in
the Papers a notice of
Wedding anniversary!
I hope you will not think
it is too late for me to
wish & offer you both our
very heartiest & most sincere
congrats on the happy anni—
versary? Yours has been
such a beautiful & ideal
marriage! it is only fitting
it should be celebrated with
much joy & thankfulness.
I have so many beautiful
& happy memories connected
with Clonbrock & with you
both. & all your unfailing
kindness to me & mine, I
should not like to be left out
in my good wishes on this
happy occasion. I hope
you are all keeping very well
& as happy as anyone can
be with this terribly bad war
raging on! I do think you
seem to be getting on at last! How
splendidly brave our men are!
My Son William had all his
three Grandsons fighting at the
front — two have now been killed
in action bravely leading their
men & only one alas! is now
left! God Grant he may be spared
to us! I saw dear Harriet & Stella
just before coming North to the
Teaching Sch. Bob did not come
to stay with us but went to Hilda's
instead — their little Granddaughter is
a great joy to them all. With love to
you & with every good wish from my husband.
